g.text: DPT per-prediction cost is low and near-flat: only ~8% growth from h=0 (1.061 ms) to h=256

text.style: font-size: 8.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans Mono', 'Bitstream Vera Sans Mono', 'Computer Modern Typewriter', 'Andale Mono', 'Nimbus Mono L', 'Courier New', 'Courier', 'Fixed', 'Terminal', monospace

g.text: (1.149 ms), the trained H_max. The fixed-h points are a pure-forward-pass LOWER BOUND (isolated

g.text: repeated calls on a frozen context snapshot); the real-episode mean sits slightly above them

text.style: font-size: 8.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans Mono', 'Bitstream Vera Sans Mono', 'Computer Modern Typewriter', 'Andale Mono', 'Nimbus Mono L', 'Courier New', 'Courier', 'Fixed', 'Terminal', monospace

g.text: because it also includes per-step context-assembly overhead (fresh tensor construction each step).

text.style: font-size: 8.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans Mono', 'Bitstream Vera Sans Mono', 'Computer Modern Typewriter', 'Andale Mono', 'Nimbus Mono L', 'Courier New', 'Courier', 'Fixed', 'Terminal', monospace

g.text: The real-episode mean, not the microbenchmark, is the number to quote for deployed cost.
